methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $195M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 12 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $300M - $18.0B.

private.name: Quantum-Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series C), post-money $8B

data.narrative:
Cape is a Series C defense and security company that has raised $195M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 12 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, comprising 2 public names (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private companies including Quantum-Systems, Helsing, Saronic, and Shield AI, with valuations ranging from $300M to $18.0B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a valuation proxy, applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement, yielding $332M. Peer-median analysis benchmarks Cape against the 12-company comp set, which carries a median valuation of $1.8B, with representative peers including Quantum-Systems at $8.0B (Series C), Helsing at $18.0B (Series D), and Dream Security at $3.0B (Series B).

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $282M (low) to $2.0B (high), with a median of $1.1B. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around Cape's relative position within a highly fragmented frontier-defense sector where comparable companies span an 60x valuation range. Key drivers of range sensitivity include technology differentiation, customer concentration, regulatory pathway clarity, and proximity to Series D readiness. Stage-progression anchors conservatively; peer-median reflects current market pricing but is heavily influenced by outlier mega-rounds at Helsing and Saronic.
